title: SAP aligns commerce data for AI personalisation

raw_body:
SAP aligns fragmented commerce data structures to enable operational AI personalisation at the execution layer. Enterprise leadership routinely establishes objectives to anticipate customer requirements and deliver relevant interactions across digital touchpoints. However, the actual infrastructure running inside these enterprises fails to support systematic execution at the required volume. Recommendation engines display generic product listings because […]
The post SAP aligns commerce data for AI personalisation appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: SAP has streamlined fragmented commerce data structures to enhance AI personalization in businesses. This change aims to help companies better anticipate customer needs and deliver tailored experiences across digital platforms. Currently, many enterprises struggle with their existing infrastructure, leading to generic product recommendations. By improving data alignment, SAP could enable more relevant interactions, making personalization more effective now.
why_it_matters: Businesses can now better meet customer expectations, leading to improved satisfaction and loyalty.
This shift signifies a broader trend towards integrating AI with operational data, enhancing overall efficiency in commerce.

lenses.eli12: SAP is making it easier for businesses to use their data to personalize customer experiences. Think of it like organizing a messy closet; when everything is in its place, you can find what you need quickly. This matters because better personalization can lead to happier customers and more sales.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this means a chance to refine user experiences by leveraging better data insights. Improved personalization can reduce costs associated with ineffective marketing strategies. Practically, businesses could see higher engagement rates as they offer more relevant product recommendations.
lenses.engineer: From a technical standpoint, SAP's approach focuses on aligning fragmented data structures to support operational AI. This could enhance the efficiency of recommendation engines, moving from generic listings to tailored suggestions. However, the article highlights that many enterprises still face challenges with their existing infrastructure.
